title: Boat Rental in Bodrum: Costs, Seasons and Top Boats for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Bodrum puts you at the gateway to the Aegean coast, where turquoise waters and pine‑clad bays await. Depart from Marina Vita or D-Marin Turgutreis for a half‑day trip costing from 3,500 TRY. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can hire a boat in Bodrum or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Bodrum?

Prices depend on boat size and season, starting around 1,500 TRY per hour for a small motorboat. You can hire a boat for a day or longer, with rates varying by type and itinerary.

Where to set off in Bodrum

Bodrum offers several well‑equipped marinas and sheltered bays for launching your trip. Marina Vita and D-Marin Turgutreis are the largest, with full services. The historic harbour at Bodrum Castle is a lively departure point for short excursions. For a quieter start, consider the bays of Yalıkavak or Torba, each with their own waterside facilities. Nearby islands such as Orak Island and Rabbit Island are favourite cruising destinations, reachable in under an hour.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat (5, 8 m), ideal for small groups exploring nearby coves; up to 6 guests; from 1,500 TRY per hour.
  2. Catamaran (12, 15 m), stable and spacious for family day trips; up to 12 guests; from 8,000 TRY half‑day.
  3. Crewed gulet (18, 25 m), traditional wooden yacht for longer cruises; up to 10 guests; from 15,000 TRY full‑day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Maiora 26m), VIP experience with crew, jacuzzi and water toys; up to 12 guests; from 30,000 TRY per day. See the Maiora 26m for hire with full specifications.
  5. Sailboat (10, 14 m), for those who want to sail; up to 8 guests; from 4,000 TRY half‑day.

Cost breakdown for Bodum boat rental

  • Shared sunset tour: 800, 1,200 TRY per person, 2‑hour trip.
  • Small motorboat half‑day: 5,000, 7,000 TRY for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full‑day: 12,000, 18,000 TRY for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full‑day: 25,000, 50,000 TRY for up to 12 guests.

To compare prices and availability, use the boat search on BoatHire24, filtering by date and boat type.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Bodrum?

For most bareboat charters you need an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent. However, many operators provide a skipper for an additional fee, removing the licence requirement.

If you book a crewed yacht the skipper knows the hidden coves around Orak Island, saving you navigation time.
For boats under a certain power the operator may waive the licence, but always confirm exact requirements when you book.

Best season for boating in Bodrum

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August being hottest and busiest. The meltemi wind can pick up in the afternoon, so morning departures are calmer. May and October offer warm water and fewer crowds. In 2026, early‑bird bookings for June slots are already recommended.

At a glance

  • Price band: 800, 50,000 TRY per trip
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: Turkish Lira (TRY)

Questions about boat rental in Bodrum

Can you rent a boat without a skipper in Bodrum?

Yes, if you hold an ICC or proven equivalent, you can charter a bareboat from most marinas.

What is the best way to find a boat for a large group?

A catamaran or crewed gulet is ideal for groups of 10, 12, offering space and comfort onboard.

Are there day trips to Greek islands from Bodrum?

Yes, some operators offer trips to Kos or other Dodecanese islands, but you need a valid passport and visa.

How far in advance should I book?

During high season, booking at least two months ahead is wise, especially for larger yachts.

Whether you are a visitor or an owner, BoatHire24 makes it simple to list your vessel or book a verified boat. If you own a boat in Bodrum, you can rent out your boat to thousands of travellers seeking a day on the water.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bodrum and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
